denominator_used: N = 54 WES samples (denominator from MOESM4 WES cohort; 49 of which have any MAF entries)
assumptions_made: WES cohort defined as 54 samples with WES=='X' in MOESM4; barcodes constructed as 'Tumor_' + WES label; non-synonymous = Missense, Nonsense, Frame_Shift_Del/Ins, Splice_Site, In_Frame_Del/Ins, Translation_Start_Site, Nonstop_Mutation (excluding Silent); FILTER=='PASS'; samples with no MAF entries counted as wild-type (not mutated) in the denominator
step_by_step_analysis:
A. DATA LOADED
- 41467_2023_38432_MOESM4_ESM.xlsx (Clinical Data): 108 rows x 15 cols; 54 rows with WES=='X'
- 41467_2023_38432_MOESM5_ESM.xlsx (MAF): 7457 rows x 123 cols

B. COHORT DEFINITION
- WES cohort: 54 samples with WES=='X' in MOESM4
- Barcodes built as 'Tumor_' + WES label -> 54 expected barcodes
- 49/54 WES barcodes found in the MAF file (5 samples had no MAF entries — treated as mutation-negative)

C. VARIABLE CODING
- Outcome: PTPRD mutation (sample-level presence)
- Filter chain:
  1. Tumor_Sample_Barcode in WES-cohort barcode set
  2. FILTER == 'PASS' (all WES-cohort rows have FILTER=='PASS')
  3. Hugo_Symbol == 'PTPRD'
  4. Variant_Classification in non-synonymous set (Missense_Mutation, Nonsense_Mutation, Frame_Shift_Del, Frame_Shift_Ins, Splice_Site, In_Frame_Del, In_Frame_Ins, Translation_Start_Site, Nonstop_Mutation) — excluding Silent

D. COMPUTATION
- After filtering: 4 PTPRD non-synonymous PASS variant rows across 4 unique samples
- Denominator: 54 (full WES cohort)
- Frequency = 4/54 = 7.41%

E. RESULT
- PTPRD mutation frequency in 54-sample WES cohort = 4/54 = 7.41%
- Matches Fig 2b reported 7%

F. CAVEATS
- 5 WES samples have no MAF entries (treated as WT for this gene); 3 MAF barcodes are not in the 54-sample WES cohort and were excluded
- Definition of 'non-synonymous' excludes Silent; if Silent were included the count would be identical because PTPRD had no Silent PASS variants anyway (verified)
- Uses sample-level (binary) call, not variant-level; a sample with multiple PTPRD variants counts once
